---
title: "Alterion Draco Brings Runtime Guardrails to Enterprise AI Agents"
date: "2026-07-17 08:00"
author: "AI News Editorial"
categories:
  - Agents & Automation
  - AI Security & Policy
summary: "Alterion launches Draco, a vendor-agnostic runtime control plane that enforces programmable guardrails on production AI agents in real time without code changes."
image: "/assets/post-images/2026-07-17-alterion-draco.svg"
---

Enterprise AI agents are moving into production across finance, HR, and customer service workflows—but operators have struggled to enforce security and compliance without rebuilding agents or locking into single vendors. Alterion's Draco platform aims to solve that.

**What happened:** Alterion announced Draco, a runtime control plane that observes prompts, actions, and payloads from production AI agents and enforces programmable guardrails in real time—without requiring any changes to the agent's underlying code.

**Why it matters:** Running agentic workflows in regulated industries means adding enforcement and auditability without sacrificing vendor flexibility. Draco intercepts high-risk actions—like data deletion, production changes, or payment execution—and can block, log, or require approval in real time. Because it works at the runtime level rather than the application level, operators don't need to rebuild agents or commit to a single model provider.

The platform supports both cloud and on-premise deployments, addressing concerns about data privacy and latency that have slowed agent adoption in financial services and healthcare.

**What's next:** Organizations running or evaluating agent deployments should map where agents perform high-risk actions and pilot runtime interception tooling. Watch for vendor-agnostic coverage claims and on-prem deployment options as differentiators when evaluating guardrail solutions.

**The bigger picture:** As autonomous agents move from pilot to production, the gap between agent capability and enterprise governance is becoming a bottleneck. Runtime control platforms like Draco represent a new category of infrastructure tooling that treats compliance and security as first-class concerns—rather than afterthoughts.
